Email infrastructure for AI agents — cre: Email infrastructure for AI agents — create inboxes, send and receive email, search, threads, and webhooks.
To get your LobsterMail API key:\n1. Sig:
To get your LobsterMail API key:
1. Sign up or log in at **https://lobstermail.ai**
2. Go to **Settings → API Keys**
3. Click **Create New Key**
4. Copy the key — it starts with `lm_sk_test_` (sandbox) or `lm_sk_live_` (production)

Need help? See https://lobstermail.ai/docs
Create a new email inbox on LobsterMail.

Permanently delete an inbox and release: Permanently delete an inbox and release its address. This action cannot be undone.
List all email inboxes on the account.

List emails in a specific inbox.

Subject: Subject
A friendly label for the inbox (e.g. \"S: A friendly label for the inbox (e.g. "Support Inbox"). Only visible inside LobsterMail.
The part before the @ in the email addre: The part before the @ in the email address (e.g. "support" → support@lobstermail.ai). Use only lowercase letters, numbers, dots, hyphens, or underscores. Leave empty to auto-generate a random address.
The domain for the inbox address. Leave: The domain for the inbox address. Leave empty to use the default lobstermail.ai. Custom domains require a Tier 2+ account and prior DNS configuration.
Select the inbox to send from. Must be a: Select the inbox to send from. Must be an active inbox on your account.
Plain text version of the email. At leas: Plain text version of the email. At least one of Plain Text or HTML body is required.
HTML version of the email. If only HTML: HTML version of the email. If only HTML is provided, a plain-text version is auto-generated.
Additional recipients to CC on the email: Additional recipients to CC on the email.
Message-ID of the email you are replying: Message-ID of the email you are replying to (for threading). You can get this from the "Get Email" action output.
ID of an existing thread to add this ema: ID of an existing thread to add this email to. Leave empty to start a new thread.
Filter by inbound (received) or outbound: Filter by inbound (received) or outbound (sent) emails.
Maximum number of emails to return (1–50: Maximum number of emails to return (1–50, default 20).
The ID of the email to retrieve (starts: The ID of the email to retrieve (starts with eml_). You can get this from the "List Emails" action or the "New Email Received" trigger output.
Text to search for. Searches email subje: Text to search for. Searches email subjects (highest priority), senders, and body previews.
Limit results to a specific inbox. Leave: Limit results to a specific inbox. Leave empty to search all inboxes.
Filter results to emails from a specific: Filter results to emails from a specific sender address (partial match supported).
Return only emails that have attachments: Return only emails that have attachments.
Maximum number of results to return (1–5: Maximum number of results to return (1–50, default 20).
Authorization headers are injected autom: Authorization headers are injected automatically from your connection.
Enable for files like PDFs, images, etc.
HEAD: HEAD
Triggers instantly when a new email is r: Triggers instantly when a new email is received in an inbox.
Triggers when a brand-new conversation (: Triggers when a brand-new conversation (thread) is started in an inbox.
Triggers when someone replies to an exis: Triggers when someone replies to an existing email thread. Useful for follow-up and support automation.
Triggers when an outbound email fails to: Triggers when an outbound email fails to deliver. Use this to update CRM records, retry with a different address, or alert your team.
Triggers when an outbound email is succe: Triggers when an outbound email is successfully delivered. Use for audit logs, CRM updates, or confirming delivery.
